findings.search_query: Jonathan Phillips 1707 Duxbury Massachusetts Mary Holmes genealogy
findings.source_title: Phillips genealogies (1885), archive.org full text; Mary (Brewster) Holmes - WikiTree
findings.snippet: Mary (Brewster) Holmes (abt.1679-1761) of Duxbury, daughter of Wrestling Brewster; children include Jonathan and others. No distinct profile located for a Jonathan Phillips b.1707 Duxbury married to a Mary Holmes.
findings.claimed_facts: Could not confirm a Jonathan Phillips born 20 Sep 1707 Duxbury married to Mary Holmes. A Mary (Brewster) Holmes of Duxbury (1679-1761) exists but is of an earlier generation; the Phillips of Duxbury/Marshfield genealogy exists but did not surface this exact person.
findings.notes: No direct match. Worklist parents (Rebecca Blaney; Thomas Haskins Phillips) and spouse Mary Holmes not corroborated by a clear source. Needs primary Duxbury VR check.

findings.search_query: Joseph Bridges 1737 York Maine Mary Polly Moody Charlotte Washington Maine
findings.source_title: Joseph Bridges (c.1737 - c.1843) - Geni
findings.snippet: Joseph Bridges was born March 13, 1737 in York, Maine and died January 30, 1830 in Charlotte, Washington, Maine. He married Polly (Mary) Moody; children included Abraham, John, Mary, Jacob, Thomas, Isaac, and William Bridges.
findings.claimed_facts: Birth 13 Mar 1737 York, Maine; death 30 Jan 1830 Charlotte, Washington, Maine; spouse Mary 'Polly' Moody. Matches worklist exactly on all recorded fields.
findings.notes: Web-tree (Geni) match agreeing fully with worklist dates/places/spouse. Geni headline shows alternate death 'c.1843' but body text gives 30 Jan 1830 matching worklist. No parents in worklist; none confirmed.

findings.search_query: Joseph Weeks 1702 Kittery York Maine Sarah Haley Nicholas Weeks genealogy
findings.source_title: Nicholas Weeks (abt.1680-1742) - WikiTree (lists son Joseph b.1702)
findings.snippet: Nicholas Weeks (c.1680-1742) of Kittery, son of Nicholas Weeks I and Judith Mendum, married Priscilla Gunnison 7 May 1700. Children: Joseph (b. July 25, 1702), Priscilla, Judith, Elizabeth, Mary. Records confirm 'Joseph, b. 1702 m. Sarah Haley.'
findings.claimed_facts: Joseph Weeks b. 25 Jul 1702 Kittery, son of Nicholas Weeks Jr and Priscilla Gunnison; married Sarah Haley (b.1709 Kittery, d. ~1748) on 23 Apr 1726. Matches worklist parents, spouse, birth date/place exactly.
findings.notes: Strong corroboration of birth date, parents, and spouse from WikiTree (citing Old Kittery and Her Families). Worklist death 'Dec 1789' not confirmed by this source.

findings.search_query: Margaret Rebecca Bartlett 1762 Truro Nova Scotia James Taylor Maugerville New Brunswick
findings.source_title: TAYLOR, JAMES (1761-1834) - Dictionary of Canadian Biography
findings.snippet: James Taylor m. Margaret Bartlett, daughter of Richard Bartlett, and they had at least four sons and two daughters. The Bartletts, having arrived from Truro around 1779, had settled in Burton, Sunbury County. Rootsweb Bartlett pages give Margaret b. 18 Aug 1762 Truro NS, d. 20 Sep 1835 Maugerville, York Co NB.
findings.claimed_facts: Margaret Bartlett, daughter of Richard Bartlett, married James Taylor; born 18 Aug 1762 Truro NS, died 20 Sep 1835 Maugerville NB. Bartlett family migrated Truro NS -> Burton/Maugerville NB. All match worklist.
findings.notes: Spouse James Taylor and father Richard Bartlett confirmed by a scholarly source (DCB). Exact birth/death dates from Rootsweb Bartlett family page (homepages.rootsweb.com/~cst/bartlett/team13a.htm), a web compilation. Worklist gives Colchester Co NS; DCB/sources say Truro (in Colchester Co) - consistent.

findings.search_query: Moses Bradbury 1715 Salisbury Massachusetts Abigail Eaton New Gloucester Maine
findings.source_title: Moses Bradbury (1715 - 1789) - Geni
findings.snippet: Moses Bradbury born November 3, 1715 in Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts; died December 1789 in New Gloucester, Cumberland, Maine. Wife Abigail; children Mary, Jacob, Hannah, Benjamin, Enos, Sarah, Moses, Samuel, Naomi, Abigail. Served as a Revolutionary patriot / tax collector in New Gloucester 1780.
findings.claimed_facts: Birth 3 Nov 1715 Salisbury MA; death 1789 New Gloucester ME; spouse Abigail. Matches worklist birth/death/places and spouse first name.
findings.notes: CONFLICT FLAG: a Salisbury VR/Geni record shows 'Abigail Eaton (b.1716) married JACOB Bradbury 18 Dec 1733' - Jacob being possibly a relative, not Moses. Whether Moses Bradbury's wife was an Eaton (as worklist states 'Abigail Eaton') needs verification; the Eaton-Bradbury marriage found was to Jacob. Parents (Elizabeth Stockman; Jacob Bradbury Sr) not independently confirmed here.

findings.search_query: Patience Titus 1730 Rehoboth Massachusetts Squire Bishop Winthrop Maine genealogy
findings.source_title: Patience Bishop (Titus) (1730 - 1802) - Geni
findings.snippet: Patience Titus born May 31, 1730 in Rehoboth, Bristol, Massachusetts; died April 21, 1802 in Winthrop, Kennebec, Maine. Parents Timothy Titus and Waitstill (Garnsey) Titus. Married Squire Bishop 18 Apr 1754; children incl. Patience, Squire, Nathaniel, Waitstill, Ann/Amy.
findings.claimed_facts: Birth 31 May 1730 Rehoboth MA; death 21 Apr 1802 Winthrop, Kennebec ME; parents Timothy Titus Sr and Waitstill Garnsey; spouse Squire Bishop (m. 1754). Matches worklist on every recorded field.
findings.notes: Full agreement with worklist (parents, spouse, dates, places) per Geni; worklist 'Kenne' is a truncation of Kennebec. Web-tree source, no primary VR cited.

findings.search_query: Robert Sedgley 1726 York Maine Bowdoinham Sagadahoc genealogy
findings.source_title: Robert Sedgley (1726 - 1796) - Geni; John Sedgley I (abt.1687-1745) - WikiTree
findings.snippet: Robert Sedgley 1726-1796; children Joseph, Stephen, Robert, Hannah, John, Elizabeth. Son of John Sedgley (bp.1687 King's Newnham, Warwickshire, son of carpenter Robert Sedgley) who married Elizabeth Adams Jan 1714 in York Village, York. Date 14 Mar 1727 in York Village given by related profiles.
findings.claimed_facts: Robert Sedgley b. ~1726/27 York, Maine; d. 1796; descendants settled in Bowdoinham, Maine; father John Sedgley I of York. Matches worklist birth place, death year, and Bowdoinham connection.
findings.notes: DATE NOTE: worklist 14 Mar 1726 vs sources 14 Mar 1727 - likely Old/New Style year difference (Mar before Mar 25). Father John Sedgley I (not in worklist) identified. Web-tree sources.

findings.search_query: Sarah Dickinson 1689 Hatfield Massachusetts John Leonard Agawam genealogy
findings.source_title: Descendants of Nathaniel Dickinson (Dickinson Assoc.), archive.org full text
findings.snippet: References to a 'Sarah (Dickinson) Leonard' among the descendants of Nathaniel Dickinson of Hatfield. The Geni 'John Leonard of Springfield' (c.1615-1676) is far too early to be the husband of a Sarah Dickinson born 1689.
findings.claimed_facts: A Sarah Dickinson of the Hatfield Dickinson family who married a Leonard is documented, consistent with worklist birthplace Hatfield. Exact 1689 birth and the specific husband 'Deacon John Leonard M.D.' not pinned down.
findings.notes: CONFLICT FLAG: worklist spouse 'Deacon John Leonard M.D.' - the prominent Geni 'John Leonard of Springfield' died 1676, impossible for a wife born 1689. The husband must be a later John Leonard. Identity/dates need the Descendants of Nathaniel Dickinson volume verified directly.

findings.search_query: Stephen Washburn 1736 Plympton Massachusetts Hannah Norris New Gloucester Maine
findings.source_title: Stephen Washburn (1736 - 1812) - Geni
findings.snippet: Stephen Washburn born September 24, 1736 in Plympton, Plymouth, Massachusetts; died February 13, 1812 in New Gloucester, Cumberland, Maine. Wife Hannah (Norris) Washburn; children Hosea, Benjamin, Stephen, Jesse, Eliphalet. Parents Ephraim Washburn and Mary (Pollard). Married 11 May 1757 in Wareham.
findings.claimed_facts: Birth 24 Sep 1736 Plympton MA; death 12/13 Feb 1812 New Gloucester ME; spouse Hannah Norris. Matches worklist exactly. Adds parents Ephraim Washburn & Mary Pollard.
findings.notes: Full agreement with worklist on dates, places, spouse. New: parents Ephraim Washburn and Mary Pollard (worklist had blank parents). Web-tree source.

findings.search_query: William Tibbetts 1731 Dover New Hampshire Benjamin Tibbetts Corinth Penobscot Maine
findings.source_title: Pvt William Tibbetts Sr (1731-1807) - FamilySearch Tree
findings.snippet: William Tibbetts born April 21, 1731 in Dover, New Hampshire; died around 1807 in Corinth, Penobscot, Maine; early settler of the Bangor area. Father Benjamin Tibbetts (b.31 Oct 1700 Dover, d.30 Jun 1761 Lebanon, York Co ME), per related WikiTree/Geni profiles.
findings.claimed_facts: Birth 21 Apr 1731 Dover NH; death ~1807 Corinth, Penobscot, Maine; father Capt Benjamin Tibbetts. Matches worklist on all recorded fields incl. father.
findings.notes: FamilySearch tree + WikiTree corroborate birth, death, and father Benjamin Tibbetts (matching worklist parent 'Capt Benjamin Tibbetts'). Note: some sources list William as a SIBLING of Benjamin rather than son - minor relationship ambiguity, but most align with Benjamin as father.

findings.search_query: Abbie Cromwell Fernald 1852 Troy Waldo Maine Joseph Fernald Simon Cromwell findagrave
findings.source_title: Simon Cromwell (1791-1884) / Joseph Fernald (1812-1862) - Find a Grave (related, not subject)
findings.snippet: Find a Grave memorials exist for Simon Cromwell (1791-1884) and Joseph Fernald (1812-1862) in the Troy/Lincolnville Maine area, but no memorial directly matching Abbie M Cromwell Fernald (1852-1934) of Troy surfaced. A cemetery listing fragment shows an 'Abigail' d. 15 Jan 1934 (unconfirmed match).
findings.claimed_facts: No confirmed record located for Abbie M Cromwell Fernald herself. Related Cromwell and Fernald individuals exist in Troy/Lincolnville, Maine. Father may be a Simon Cromwell of Maine.
findings.notes: No direct hit. Worklist father 'Simon Cromwell' b.1813 differs from the Find a Grave Simon Cromwell b.1791 (could be father vs another) - relationship unverified. Recommend direct Find a Grave / Maine VR search.

findings.person_name: Emma Mary or Mary Emma Knowles
findings.search_query: Emma Knowles Bagley 1846 Calais Maine John Bagley Reuben Knowles Stacyville Maine
findings.source_title: (no matching record located)
findings.claimed_facts: No confirming free-web source located for Emma/Mary Knowles (b.1846) wife of Jonathan/John H Bagley, daughter of Reuben Knowles and Elizabeth Sprague.
findings.notes: Searched FamilySearch/WikiTree/archive.org Knowles family of Eastham and Bagley genealogy; no clear match. Note worklist birthplace 'Calais, Waldo, Maine' is questionable - Calais is in Washington County, not Waldo. Possible place error to flag.

findings.search_query: Gertrude Evelyn Bridges Danforth 1873 Mars Hill Maine Otis Danforth Troy Waldo Maine
findings.source_title: (no matching record located)
findings.claimed_facts: No confirming free-web source located for Gertrude Evelyn Bridges (1873-1939), wife of Otis B Danforth, daughter of Joseph Reuben Bridges and Hannah Joan Wood.
findings.notes: No hit. A Find a Grave 'Gertrude Frances Ives Bridges (1887-1941)' of Owego NY is a different person (do not conflate). Recommend Find a Grave Troy, Waldo Co ME cemetery browse + Maine VR.

findings.search_query: Mary L Gilman Bragg Albion Kennebec Maine John Taylor Gilman 1833 1888
findings.source_title: (no direct record located; peripheral: John Taylor Gilman M.D. catalog)
findings.snippet: A HathiTrust catalog record 'John Taylor Gilman, M. D., Portland Maine' exists, relating to her named father Dr. John Taylor Gilman, but no record directly documenting Mary L Gilman (abt.1833, d.1888 Albion) wife of Salatheal Leonard Bragg was found.
findings.claimed_facts: No direct confirmation of Mary L Gilman herself. Father Dr. John Taylor Gilman is a documented Maine physician (separate worklist person).
findings.notes: No hit for the subject. CONFLICT NOTE for her father: a source gives 'John Taylor Gilman 1806-1884' whereas worklist death is 1903 - possibly two different J.T. Gilmans or a date error; relevant to confirming Mary's parentage.

findings.search_query: Otis J Danforth Nora Gerry Maine Otis B Danforth 1853 genealogy
findings.source_title: (no matching record located)
findings.claimed_facts: No confirming free-web source located for Otis B Danforth (abt.1871-1941) of Troy, Waldo, Maine, husband of Gertrude Evelyn Bridges, son of Otis J Danforth and Nora E Danforth (Gerry).
findings.notes: No hit. Recommend Find a Grave Troy, Waldo Co ME and 1900/1910/1940 census for Otis B Danforth.

findings.search_query: (insufficient identifying data; not searched in depth)
findings.source_title: (no matching record located)
findings.claimed_facts: No source located. Worklist provides only birth year 1831, no places or dates; spouse 'George DeGrasses/Grass Jr'; parents 'John Everet' and 'Clarisa Daniel'.
findings.notes: Insufficient identifying detail (no birth/death place or date) to perform a reliable disambiguated search; surname spelling unstable (Grass/DeGrasse). Needs more data before web research is productive.

findings.search_query: Captain David Ives 1740 Wallingford Connecticut Eunice Gillett Burton Sunbury New Brunswick
findings.source_title: David Ives (1740-1820) - WikiTree
findings.snippet: David Ives born 15 Jun 1740 Wallingford, New Haven, Connecticut; parents Benjamin Ives and Hannah (Moss) Atwater. Married Eunice Gillett Feb/25 Mar 1761. Biography: 'He died in 1821 in Burton, New Brunswick, Canada.' Corporal in Lexington Alarm 1775, later in Col. Seth Warner's Green Mountain Boys; buried Morrison Cemetery, Burton, Sunbury NB.
findings.claimed_facts: Birth 15 Jun 1740 Wallingford CT; parents Benjamin Ives & Hannah Moss; spouse Eunice Gillett; died 1820/1821 Burton, Sunbury, New Brunswick. Matches worklist on every field incl. parents and spouse.
findings.notes: Strong agreement with worklist (birth, parents, spouse, NB death). Minor conflict: WikiTree data field says died Wallingford CT while the biography text and burial say Burton NB - worklist's NB is consistent with the narrative/burial. Revolutionary service noted (potential SAR/DAR lead).

findings.search_query: Daniel Winne 1739 Albany New York Catherine Houghtaling Sharon Springs Schoharie
findings.source_title: Winne Genealogy / New Netherland Settlers - WikiTree
findings.snippet: Multiple Daniel Winne profiles in the Albany Dutch Winne family (e.g., baptized 20 Nov 1720 Reformed Dutch Church Albany). A 'Genealogy of the Houghtaling-Stifflear Families of Schoharie County, New York' (Stifflear) documents Houghtaling-Winne connections in Schoharie. No profile cleanly matching Daniel b.10 Jan 1739 Albany, husband of Catherine/Tryntje Houghtaling, isolated.
findings.claimed_facts: The Winne family of Albany and Houghtaling family of Schoharie are well documented and intermarried, consistent with worklist, but a specific Daniel Winne b.1739 m. Catherine Houghtaling was not confirmed.
findings.notes: Context supports plausibility (Dutch Winne/Houghtaling in Albany-Schoharie) but no exact match. The Stifflear Houghtaling-Schoharie genealogy (werelate.org source) is a promising lead to verify the Catherine Houghtaling marriage.

findings.search_query: Eunice Gillett Ives 1741 Windsor Connecticut Burton New Brunswick findagrave Morrison cemetery
findings.source_title: Descendants PAF page (brentglad.com); corroborated by WikiTree Ives-88
findings.snippet: Eunice Gillett born 6 Sep 1741, christened 6 Nov 1741, Bloomfield (Wintonbury/Windsor), Hartford, Connecticut. Married David Ives (son of Benjamin Ives and Hannah Moss) 25 Mar 1761 in Goshen, Litchfield, CT. Died in Burton, Sunbury, New Brunswick; buried Morrison Cemetery, Burton, Sunbury, NB.
findings.claimed_facts: Birth 6 Sep 1741 (chr. 6 Nov 1741) Windsor/Bloomfield CT; spouse David Ives; died Burton, Sunbury NB; buried Morrison Cemetery. Matches worklist spouse and birthplace; worklist 'death 13 Jan 1804 Granby, Hartford CT' conflicts.
findings.notes: BIRTH/DEATH NOTES: Worklist birth '06 Nov 1741' matches the christening date (actual birth 6 Sep 1741). CONFLICT: worklist death '13 Jan 1804 Granby, Hartford, CT' contradicts sources placing her death/burial in Burton, Sunbury, New Brunswick (with husband David Ives). Worklist death likely belongs to a different Eunice Gillett - flag for review.

findings.search_query: Rebecca Vickary Vickery William Daniel Alloway Edward Vickery New Jersey
findings.source_title: Joel Daniel UE (1759-1807) - WikiTree (son of William Daniel & Rebecca Vickery)
findings.snippet: William Daniel (1721-1764) married Rebecca Vickery; they had 8 children. Joel Daniel born 19 Oct 1759, probably in Alloway, New Jersey, to William Daniel and Rebecca Vickery; William died 1764, his will naming Joel and seven other children.
findings.claimed_facts: Rebecca/Rebeckah Vickery married William Daniel of Alloway, New Jersey; family had ~8 children; William d.1764. Confirms worklist spouse 'William Daniel' and Alloway NJ connection.
findings.notes: Spouse and NJ/Alloway location confirmed via WikiTree (citing William Daniel's 1764 will). Worklist birth 13 Dec 1725 and parents (Edward Vickery; Sarah Smart/Firth) not directly confirmed in this source. WikiTree gives William b.1721 (worklist had no Daniel birth).
